features.description: CRITICAL: Remove TrackNavigator auto-navigation component. components/TrackNavigator.tsx is mounted in app/layout.tsx (line 78). It has a useEffect that watches currentTrack.slug and isPlaying — whenever a NEW track starts playing, it does router.push(`/track/${slug}`), which navigates the user away from the album page to the track page. This is the ROOT CAUSE of the play-then-navigate bug. The user clicks a track on the album page expecting it to play in-place, but TrackNavigator forces navigation to /track/[slug]. Fix: Delete components/TrackNavigator.tsx entirely. Remove the import and <TrackNavigator /> from app/layout.tsx (around line 6 and line 78). Users should stay on whatever page they're on when they play a song — they can navigate to the track page by clicking the ChevronRight icon or the NowPlayingBar. DONE: TrackNavigator.tsx is deleted. No import or usage in layout.tsx. Playing a song from the album page does NOT navigate away. DO NOT TOUCH: NowPlayingBar (it has a Link and that's fine — user explicitly clicks it). VERIFY: pnpm build passes. No references to TrackNavigator in any file.
steps: Delete components/TrackNavigator.tsx
Remove import TrackNavigator from app/layout.tsx
Remove <TrackNavigator /> JSX from app/layout.tsx
Verify no remaining references: grep -r TrackNavigator in the project
pnpm build passes

features.description: Fix play/navigate on topics track list. app/topics/[tag]/page.tsx wraps entire track rows in a <Link>, making it impossible to play without navigating. Apply the same play-first pattern: row click plays the song, small icon for navigation. This is currently a server component — it needs to become a client component. Since generateStaticParams and generateMetadata cannot be in client components, split into page.tsx (server, keeps metadata/params) and a new TopicPageClient.tsx component. The audio context has a playTrack method — use it. Check lib/audio-context.tsx for the correct method name to play a single track by its data. If no direct playTrack method exists, use playAlbum(track.albumSlug, trackIndex) to play. DONE: Clicking a track on /topics/[slug] plays the song. A small ChevronRight navigates to the track page. Locked tracks (no audioUrl) show but don't play on click. DO NOT TOUCH: /topics page itself, lib/ files. VERIFY: pnpm build passes. /topics/[tag] pages still generate statically.
steps: Read app/topics/[tag]/page.tsx
Read lib/audio-context.tsx to find the correct play method
Create app/topics/[tag]/TopicPageClient.tsx as a 'use client' component
Move the track list rendering into TopicPageClient, accepting tracks and category as props
Import useAudio, useRouter in TopicPageClient
Replace Link wrappers with div onClick that plays the track using audio context
Add ChevronRight icon button for navigation with stopPropagation
Keep page.tsx as server component with generateStaticParams/generateMetadata, render TopicPageClient
pnpm build passes

features.description: Add author/source attribution to album track rows. Each track has a sources array with { title, type, url, guest }. Below the genre/mood line on unlocked tracks, add a new attribution line showing the primary source guest name and source type. Format: small icon (Mic icon for podcast, FileText icon for newsletter) + guest name, all in muted styling. This connects each song to its Lenny source content. DONE: Every unlocked track row shows guest name with a type icon below the genre line. Locked tracks show concept only (no change). DO NOT TOUCH: track pages, homepage, NowPlayingBar, lib/ files. VERIFY: All unlocked tracks display attribution. Icons correctly reflect source type (podcast vs newsletter). pnpm build passes.
steps: Read app/album/[slug]/AlbumPageClient.tsx
Import Mic and FileText icons from lucide-react

After the genre/mood

tag and tags div in unlocked tracks, add a new attribution line
Use track.sources[0] for the primary source — show type icon + guest name
Style: text-[10px] text-muted-foreground/40 flex items-center gap-1 mt-1
Handle empty sources array gracefully (don't render attribution line)
pnpm build passes

features.description: Add 'Based on' attribution card to track detail pages. On TrackPageClient.tsx, after the lyrics/SyncedLyrics section, add a 'Based on' section that links to the original Lenny newsletter or podcast episode. For each source in track.sources, show: source title (as external link), guest name, type badge ('Newsletter' or 'Podcast' in a small pill), and the URL. Style the card like the existing annotation panels: accent color left border (3px solid), subtle background (bg-white/5 or bg-black/[0.02]), rounded, font-lenny for the title. If multiple sources exist, show all. DONE: Every track page has a 'Based on' section showing source articles/podcasts with links. DO NOT TOUCH: album pages, homepage, audio player. VERIFY: Card renders on all track pages. External links open in new tab. pnpm build passes.
steps: Read app/track/[slug]/TrackPageClient.tsx to find the right insertion point
After the SyncedLyrics component or lyrics section, add a 'Based on' heading and cards
For each source: render title as <a> link, guest name, type pill badge
Style with accent left border, subtle bg, font-lenny for title text
External links: target=_blank rel=noopener noreferrer
pnpm build passes

features.description: Add Genius-style annotations for all Founders album tracks (4 tracks). Currently only do-things-that-dont-scale has annotations. Add 3-5 annotations each to: product-market-fit, the-maze, taste, eleven-stars. Use each track's lyrics, concept, storyBehind, keyInsight, and sources to write factual annotations explaining what specific lyric lines reference. Each annotation object: { lyricText: string (must match a real line in the lyrics, case-insensitive partial match), note: string (the explanation — specific, factual, interesting), sourceTitle: string, sourceUrl: string }. DONE: All 5 Founders tracks have annotations arrays with 3-5 items each in lib/albums.ts. DO NOT TOUCH: component files, other album tracks. VERIFY: pnpm build passes. Each lyricText substring exists in that track's lyrics.
steps: Read lib/albums.ts foundersTracks section — focus on lyrics for each track
For product-market-fit: identify 3-5 lyric lines referencing Rahul Vohra, superhuman, PMF survey, etc. Write annotations.
For the-maze: identify lines referencing tarpit ideas, Dalton Caldwell advice, etc. Write annotations.
For taste: identify lines referencing Jony Ive, Dieter Rams, craft, etc. Write annotations.
For eleven-stars: identify lines referencing Chesky's 11-star framework. Write annotations.
Add annotations arrays to each track object in lib/albums.ts
pnpm build passes
